answer:
\boxed{D}

A fully diminished seventh chord (e.g., **diminished triad + minor seventh**) has all three inversions that sound essentially the same due to the tritone substitution and voice-leading properties of the diminished scale. This is unique among the options, as the other chords (minor triad, half-diminished seventh, and diminished triad) have distinct harmonic functions or voicings in their inversions.
